items.title: Inline Sync Annotations
items.description: Special comment syntax in CLAUDE.md like `<!-- harness:exclude -->` or `<!-- harness:only=codex,gemini -->` to control what gets synced at the section or line level. Users need fine-grained control — some rules are Claude Code-specific (using Claude-only tools) and shouldn't pollute other harnesses. Solves the all-or-nothing sync problem without requiring separate config files.

items.title: Import From Target Harnesses
items.description: Reverse sync: read settings, rules, or agents from Gemini/Codex/OpenCode and propose merging them back into CLAUDE.md. Teams often configure harnesses directly and then want to canonicalize. Solves the 'I tweaked my Gemini config but now it's out of sync with Claude Code' problem by making the flow bidirectional.

items.title: Named Sync Profiles
items.description: Define named profiles (work, personal, client-acme) with different targets, exclusions, and overrides per profile. Activate with `/sync --profile=work`. Users have fundamentally different needs across contexts — work projects need company MCP servers synced, personal projects don't. Eliminates the need to manually reconfigure before syncing in different contexts.

items.title: Conflict Resolution Wizard
items.description: When a target harness has manual edits that differ from what HarnessSync would generate, show a diff and offer three-way merge options: keep mine, keep theirs, or merge. Currently, re-syncing silently overwrites user edits in target configs. This is a major trust issue that prevents users from touching target configs at all.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Layer
items.description: A `.harnesssync/overrides/gemini.md` file that gets appended/merged into the synced output for that harness only, without touching CLAUDE.md. Preserves the single-source-of-truth model while allowing harness-specific additions. Solves the tension between canonical config and harness-specific tuning.

items.title: GitHub Actions Sync on PR Merge
items.description: Official GitHub Action that auto-syncs config files in a repo when CLAUDE.md changes are merged to main. Teams using shared CLAUDE.md in a monorepo get their Codex/Gemini configs auto-updated as PRs land. Solves the 'we updated CLAUDE.md but forgot to re-run sync' problem on shared repos.

items.title: Cross-Machine Sync via Git
items.description: Use a private git repo as the sync state backend so your Claude Code config stays consistent across multiple dev machines. When you update skills on your home machine, they propagate to your work machine via the git backend. Solves the 'I configured my laptop but not my desktop' problem without requiring manual file copying.

items.title: Secret & Sensitive Path Detector
items.description: Before syncing, scan config files for API keys, tokens, hardcoded paths, and internal hostnames. Block the sync and warn the user rather than spreading secrets to multiple config files. Solves accidental credential leakage — users often paste working commands with tokens into CLAUDE.md without thinking about where they'll propagate.

items.title: Live Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Interactive table showing which features of your current config will sync cleanly, partially, or not at all to each target harness — updated in real time as you edit CLAUDE.md. Users discover sync failures only after they run `/sync`. This surfaces the gaps proactively, before they invest time configuring something that won't translate.

items.title: First-Run Setup Wizard
items.description: On first install, auto-detect installed harnesses, ask a few questions about sync preferences, and configure everything with no manual JSON editing. Most users abandon config tools before the first successful sync because setup is opaque. A guided wizard converts installs into active users.

items.title: Harness Tone/Style Translation
items.description: Automatically rewrite CLAUDE.md instructions to match each harness's preferred style — Gemini works better with structured headers, Codex prefers terse bullet points. The same instruction written for Claude Code often performs poorly in other harnesses because of prompt sensitivity. Translation improves actual AI behavior at targets, not just config format.

items.title: Skill Gap Report
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills and agents have no functional equivalent in each target harness, and suggest workarounds or simplified replacements. Users invest heavily in Claude Code skills, then discover half of them are inert in Codex. A gap report sets expectations and guides users toward more portable skill design.

items.title: Cursor & Windsurf Adapters
items.description: Extend sync to IDE-embedded AI harnesses: sync rules to Cursor's `.cursorrules`, Windsurf's config, and Copilot instructions. Developers using IDE harnesses alongside CLI harnesses have no way to keep rules in sync. This closes the biggest gap in harness coverage and reaches a much larger user segment.

items.title: Aider Config Adapter
items.description: Sync relevant Claude Code config to Aider's `.aider.conf.yml` and `CONVENTIONS.md`. Aider has a substantial user base with its own conventions. Adding an Aider adapter directly expands the addressable audience for HarnessSync without changing the core architecture.

items.title: Automatic Versioned Backups
items.description: Before each sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs into a dated backup directory. `/sync-restore 2026-03-10` rolls back any harness to a previous state. Users fear syncing because there's no undo. Automatic backups make sync feel safe, removing the psychological barrier to using it.

items.title: CLAUDE.md Portability Score
items.description: Score your CLAUDE.md on cross-harness portability (0–100), with specific suggestions: 'This MCP tool reference is Claude-only — add a fallback for other harnesses.' Users write CLAUDE.md for Claude Code without considering portability. A score with actionable advice guides them toward configs that work everywhere.

items.title: Team Baseline Templates
items.description: Publish and consume versioned team config templates that define the canonical CLAUDE.md + sync rules for a project. New teammates run `/sync-setup --from=template:acme-backend` to bootstrap their entire setup. Onboarding to a team's AI tooling takes hours; templates compress it to minutes.

items.title: Auto-Sync on Git Pull
items.description: Git hook that detects when a pull/merge changes CLAUDE.md and automatically triggers sync. When teams update shared config, each developer must remember to re-run sync manually — they don't. A post-merge hook makes sync ambient and eliminates config drift on team repos.

items.title: Drift Monitoring & Alerts
items.description: Background process (or cron) that periodically checks if target harness configs have drifted from what HarnessSync would generate, and sends a desktop notification or Slack message. Drift accumulates silently over days/weeks as users manually edit target files. Alerts surface drift before it causes inconsistent AI behavior.

items.title: MCP Server Availability Validator
items.description: Before syncing MCP server configs to a target, verify each server is installed and reachable in the target environment. Users add MCP servers to Claude Code that aren't installed on all machines or in all harnesses. Syncing broken MCP refs causes silent failures in target harnesses — this surfaces them immediately.

items.title: Natural Language Sync Rules
items.description: Describe sync behavior in plain English: 'Don't sync any MCP servers to Gemini, but sync all skills' — HarnessSync generates the corresponding config. The sync rule DSL is a learning curve for new users. NL input lowers the barrier and makes configuration feel like a conversation rather than a spec.

items.title: VS Code Status Bar Extension
items.description: VS Code extension with a status bar indicator showing sync health (green/yellow/red), a one-click sync button, and inline diff viewer for target configs. Developers spend most time in VS Code, not in terminals. Surfacing HarnessSync status and controls in the IDE removes the context switch to run sync commands.

items.title: Sync Changelog Generator
items.description: After each sync, auto-generate a human-readable changelog entry: 'Added 2 skills, updated MCP permissions for Gemini, removed deprecated rule from Codex.' Creates a running log of configuration evolution. Users have no visibility into what changed when across their harness configs — this gives them a history they can reason about.

items.title: Per-Project Sync Config
items.description: HarnessSync config at the project level (`.harnesssync.json` in repo root) overrides global sync behavior for that project — different targets, exclusions, or profiles per repo. A developer's work repo needs different sync rules than their personal projects. Project-scoped config eliminates the need to reconfigure globally when switching contexts.

items.title: Harness Task Performance Comparison
items.description: Run the same benchmark task across all configured harnesses and report which performs best on your codebase. After syncing, users don't know which harness to use for which task. Performance data drives confident harness selection — 'Use Codex for refactoring, Gemini for documentation on this repo.'

items.title: Sync Event Webhooks
items.description: Post a JSON payload to a configurable URL on sync events (success, failure, drift detected). Pipe sync events to Slack, Discord, or a custom dashboard. Teams using shared config repos have no observability into when syncs run or fail. Webhooks enable team-level monitoring without building custom tooling.

items.title: Sync Complexity Analyzer
items.description: Flag config sections that are expensive or risky to sync — deeply nested MCP configs, ambiguous permissions, non-portable tool references — with plain-English explanations. Users don't know why some things sync perfectly and others silently break. Complexity analysis builds mental models and guides better config authoring.

items.title: Task-to-Harness Recommender
items.description: Given a description of what you're about to do (refactor, debug, write docs), recommend the best-configured harness to open. Different harnesses have different strengths after sync — some skills only work in Claude Code, some MCP tools only work in Gemini. Recommendations remove the decision fatigue of choosing a harness each time.

items.title: README Sync Status Badge
items.description: Generate a Shields.io-style badge showing sync health and supported harnesses for a project's README. Teams sharing repos want to signal 'this repo is configured for Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini.' A badge provides social proof and helps new contributors understand the project's AI tooling at a glance.

items.title: Primary Harness Migration Guide
items.description: When a user wants to switch primary harness (e.g., Claude Code → Gemini), generate a step-by-step migration plan with config translations, feature gaps, and workflow adjustments. Switching primary harness is daunting because of config investment. A migration guide makes the transition tractable and positions HarnessSync as a neutral multi-harness tool.

items.title: Claude Code Plugin Compatibility Registry
items.description: A browsable registry of Claude Code plugins annotated with which features sync to which harnesses. Users install plugins without knowing which capabilities will work in their non-Claude harnesses. A compatibility registry prevents disappointment and helps users make informed plugin installation decisions.

items.title: Sandbox Sync Simulator
items.description: Simulate what a sync would produce in a temp directory without touching real config files — shows rendered output for each target. Useful for testing config changes before committing. Users are afraid to experiment with CLAUDE.md because they can't preview effects. A simulator makes experimentation safe.

items.title: Environment Variable Portability Advisor
items.description: Analyze env vars referenced in your config and flag those that only exist in your current environment (shell-specific, machine-specific). Show which targets will break if the var is missing. ENV vars are a silent failure mode — configs reference vars that don't exist in the target context, causing cryptic failures.

items.title: Harness Version Compatibility Checker
items.description: Detect the installed version of each target harness and warn when HarnessSync is generating config features not supported by that version. Harnesses update frequently with breaking changes. Version-aware generation prevents syncing config syntax that the installed version can't parse.

items.title: Cherry-Pick Partial Sync
items.description: Sync only specific items: `/sync --only=skills:code-review,mcp:github` instead of full sync. When iterating on a single skill, full sync is wasteful and risky. Cherry-picking makes HarnessSync fit into tight iteration loops and reduces fear of unintended side effects.

items.title: Cross-Harness Config Formatter/Linter
items.description: Lint CLAUDE.md and flag issues that will cause poor or broken sync: unclosed tags, missing newlines, over-long sections that exceed target limits, harness-specific syntax. Users author CLAUDE.md for Claude Code without awareness of downstream constraints. Linting catches problems at author time instead of sync time.

items.title: MCP Tool Stub Generation for Non-MCP Harnesses
items.description: When syncing to harnesses without MCP support, auto-generate shell script stubs that approximate MCP tool behavior (e.g., a bash script that wraps the same underlying API). Users who rely on MCP tools in Claude Code get at least degraded-but-functional equivalents elsewhere.

items.title: MCP Tool Shim Generator
items.description: For MCP tools unsupported in a target harness, generate a stub skill/command that explains the capability gap and suggests a manual workaround. Instead of silently missing a tool, the user gets a helpful prompt explaining what's unavailable and why.

items.title: MCP Tool Allowlist Bridge
items.description: Automatically translate Claude Code's MCP tool allowlists/denylists into each harness's equivalent permission model (Codex tool permissions, Gemini tool access config). Permission mismatches are a top source of 'why does this work in Claude but not Gemini?' confusion.

items.title: Interactive Conflict Resolver
items.description: When a target harness has local modifications that diverge from Claude Code's source of truth, surface a diff-based UI in the terminal letting users choose per-section: keep local, accept source, or merge. Currently drift is silently overwritten, which destroys intentional per-harness customizations users made directly in Codex or OpenCode.

items.title: Dry-Run Preview Mode
items.description: Add a `/sync --dry-run` flag that shows exactly what would change across all targets without writing anything. Displays a color-coded diff per target file. Users are afraid to run sync on production setups without knowing what will change — this removes that anxiety and builds trust in the tool.

items.title: Harness Health Dashboard
items.description: A `/sync-health` command that checks whether each target harness is installed, authenticated, and functional — not just whether config files exist. Shows version info, detected capabilities, and known compatibility issues. Users waste time debugging sync failures that are actually 'Gemini CLI not installed' situations.

items.title: Selective Sync Rules
items.description: Let users annotate CLAUDE.md sections with tags like `<!-- sync:exclude -->` or `<!-- sync:codex-only -->` to control what gets synced where. Some rules are Claude Code-specific (referencing MCP tools that don't exist in Codex) and blindly syncing them pollutes other harnesses with unusable instructions.

items.title: Reverse Sync Import
items.description: A `/sync-import` command that reads config from a target harness (Codex AGENTS.md, OpenCode skills) and merges useful content back into Claude Code. Users who started on Codex before discovering Claude Code have existing institutional knowledge trapped in AGENTS.md that they can't easily migrate.

items.title: Sync Profiles
items.description: Named sync profiles (e.g., 'work', 'oss', 'client-x') that control which accounts and targets are active per project. Switch with `/sync --profile work`. Users who consult for multiple clients need completely different sync targets per project and currently have to manually reconfigure every time.

items.title: MCP Capability Compatibility Matrix
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically detect which servers have native equivalents or partial support in each target harness, and surface a compatibility report. Users don't know which of their 20 MCP servers will actually work in Gemini CLI vs. silently fail — this removes the guesswork.

items.title: Auto-Sync on CLAUDE.md Save
items.description: Extend the existing PostToolUse hook to also trigger sync when Claude Code writes CLAUDE.md or settings.json via file-watching (fswatch/inotify). The current hook only fires on Claude Code tool use, missing the case where users edit config files directly in their editor.

items.title: Skill Coverage Gap Detector
items.description: Analyze which Claude Code skills have no equivalent in target harnesses and surface these as 'coverage gaps' with suggestions for how to approximate them. Users assume their Claude Code workflows are replicated everywhere but discover gaps only when they switch harnesses mid-task.

items.title: Sync Changelog Feed
items.description: Maintain a human-readable `.harness-sync/changelog.md` that logs every sync event with timestamps, what changed, and which targets were updated. Users in teams need an audit trail of when config changed and why — currently sync is a black box with no history.

items.title: Team Config Sharing via Git
items.description: A `/sync-export` command that generates a portable, shareable `.harness-sync/team-config.json` that teammates can import to replicate your multi-account setup. Onboarding new team members to the same harness configuration currently requires manual documentation and tribal knowledge.

items.title: Cross-Harness Prompt Benchmarker
items.description: Run a canned prompt against multiple harnesses simultaneously and show response quality, latency, and cost side-by-side. Helps users decide which harness to use for which task category. Users have no data-driven way to evaluate whether their Claude Code investment actually performs better than Gemini or Codex for specific workflows.

items.title: Permission Model Advisor
items.description: Detect when Claude Code's allow/deny tool permissions have no equivalent in a target harness and proactively warn with specific advice (e.g., 'Codex has no bash restriction equivalent — consider adding a guard comment in AGENTS.md'). Currently permission drift is invisible and creates security inconsistencies.

items.title: Harness Usage Analytics
items.description: Track locally (no telemetry) which harnesses are actually being used and how often, then surface this in `/sync-status` to help users decide which targets to keep syncing. Users often have 4 harnesses configured but only use 2 — the dead weight creates noise and slows sync.

items.title: Conditional Sync Triggers
items.description: Let users configure sync to trigger only under specific conditions: only on git branch changes, only when certain files change, or only at specific times. Power users want surgical control over when expensive sync operations fire rather than syncing on every Claude Code file write.

items.title: Skill Translation Engine
items.description: Intelligently translate Claude Code skill markdown into target-native formats, stripping Claude Code-specific tool calls and rewriting them as generic instructions. Currently skills are copied verbatim, leaving Codex with references to Bash/Edit/Write tools it doesn't understand.

items.title: New Harness Auto-Detector
items.description: Periodically scan PATH and common install locations for newly installed AI coding CLIs and prompt the user to add them as sync targets. Users install Windsurf or Cursor-CLI and forget to add it to HarnessSync — they only notice weeks later when it's hopelessly out of date.

items.title: Config Snapshot & Restore
items.description: Before every sync, snapshot the current state of all target configs so users can roll back with `/sync-restore --date 2026-03-10`. Users who push a bad CLAUDE.md change have no way to recover target harness configs that were overwritten.

items.title: Environment Variable Propagation
items.description: Sync relevant environment variables (API keys, tool paths, custom endpoints) across harness config files where the target format supports it, with warnings when a variable has no target equivalent. Users repeatedly re-enter the same API keys in every harness's settings.

items.title: Pre-Commit Sync Gate
items.description: A git pre-commit hook installer that runs sync-status before commits and fails if any target is out of sync, preventing commits with stale harness configs. Teams who review each other's CLAUDE.md changes need assurance that Codex and Gemini are always current.

items.title: Per-Harness Override Files
items.description: Support `.harness-sync/overrides/codex.md` files that get appended to the synced AGENTS.md for Codex-specific instructions. Users need to add harness-specific context without polluting CLAUDE.md — currently they're forced to either duplicate or go without.

items.title: AI-Powered Rule Optimizer
items.description: Use Claude to analyze CLAUDE.md and suggest which rules are redundant, conflicting, or too verbose for target harnesses, then offer to auto-optimize. Rules accumulate over time and many become outdated or contradictory — users have no systematic way to audit them.

items.title: Sync Notification Webhooks
items.description: After sync, POST a summary payload to a configurable webhook URL (Slack, Discord, custom endpoint). Teams using shared Claude Code configs in a repo need visibility when someone updates the rules that affect everyone's harness.

items.title: Feature Parity Report
items.description: A `/sync-parity` command that produces a structured report of every Claude Code feature in use (MCP servers, skills, rules, agents) with a per-target compatibility score and specific gaps. Gives users a clear picture of what they're giving up when working in a non-Claude harness.

items.title: Project-Aware Sync Scope
items.description: Detect the current git repo and automatically scope sync to project-level configs only when inside a project, falling back to global when at home. Users constantly forget to specify scope and accidentally sync global rules when they meant project-only.

items.title: Pre-Sync Config Linter
items.description: Before syncing, validate that CLAUDE.md and settings.json are well-formed, check for common mistakes (broken skill references, invalid JSON, missing required fields), and refuse to sync invalid configs with actionable error messages. Bad configs silently propagate to all targets and are hard to debug.

items.title: Terminal Status Bar Badge
items.description: A lightweight daemon that displays sync drift status in shell prompt or tmux status bar (e.g., `[HS: 2 stale]`). Users only discover drift when things break — ambient visibility means they address it proactively.

items.title: Bulk Account Onboarding Wizard
items.description: An interactive wizard that discovers all installed harnesses, detects existing account configurations, and sets up multi-account sync in one guided session instead of per-harness manual configuration. The current setup process requires separate steps per harness and account, discouraging full adoption.

items.title: MCP Server Registry Lookup
items.description: When syncing MCP servers, automatically check a community registry for target-native alternatives (e.g., 'The GitHub MCP you use has a Gemini-native extension — want to use that instead?'). Users get better harness-native behavior rather than generic wrapped servers.

items.title: Scheduled Sync with Cron Integration
items.description: Allow users to schedule automatic syncs at off-peak times using system cron or HarnessSync's built-in scheduler. Users who make many rapid config changes don't want sync to fire on every change but do want eventual consistency without manual intervention.

items.title: Cross-Harness Secret Sanitizer
items.description: Scan source configs for patterns matching secrets (API keys, tokens, passwords) before syncing and either redact them or warn the user. Users accidentally commit API keys into CLAUDE.md which then get propagated to AGENTS.md that may be checked into version control.

items.title: Harness Adoption Coach
items.description: After sync, surface a tip about a Claude Code feature the user has configured that they haven't enabled in other harnesses yet, with a one-click setup. Users don't know what they're missing across harnesses — proactive coaching drives deeper adoption.

items.title: Config Evolution Graph
items.description: Track how CLAUDE.md and target configs have evolved over time and visualize the history in a terminal-friendly graph with `/sync-history`. Users working on long-lived projects want to understand when and why their config grew, and which rules have been stable vs. volatile.

items.title: Sync Self-Test Suite
items.description: A `/sync-test` command that creates isolated temp directories, runs a full sync cycle with fixture configs, and verifies correctness without touching real harness configs. Power users and contributors need a safe way to validate sync behavior after changes.

items.title: Community Rules Hub
items.description: A curated directory of shareable CLAUDE.md rule packs (e.g., 'TypeScript Best Practices', 'Python TDD Pack') that users can pull directly into their config with `/sync-install typescript-rules`. Users spend hours writing rules from scratch that the community has already solved.

items.title: Consider consolidating sync-* commands (5 commands)
items.description: There are 5 commands with prefix "sync-": sync-health.md, sync-parity.md, sync-restore.md, sync-setup.md, sync-status.md. Consider whether some could be subcommands of a single parent command for a cleaner UX.

items.title: MCP Tool Compatibility Matrix
items.description: Show which MCP tools work in which harnesses and flag tools that will silently fail. Users waste hours debugging why an MCP capability works in Claude Code but not in Gemini.
